coverage_analysis.note: 24 unique SOT files directly referenced/checked across Phase 3 agents 1-9. The remaining 44 files are primarily STEP7 guides (16), STEP7 reviews (8), STEP7 specs (6), and supplementary documents. These are V2/V3 enhancement specs less relevant to PART2 V0/V1 core verification.

under_checked.reason: No Phase 3 agent explicitly verified D2.0-04 claims. PART2 references it 329 times (within D2.0-0X group) but no agent focused on Infra Core independently.
under_checked.risk: MEDIUM — Infra Core defines BrainAdapter, RateLimitConfig, PromptCacheManager schemas

under_checked.reason: Schema details for D4 (ToolRegistryEntry, BrainAdapterResponse, etc.) not directly cross-checked by any Phase 3 agent
under_checked.risk: LOW — field counts are in PART2 schema tables but source verification missing

under_checked.reason: Agent 7 checked D2.0-07 (design) but not the schema document D2.1-D7 directly
under_checked.risk: LOW — schemas referenced in PART2 tables

under_checked.reason: No agent verified D2.1-D8 schema compliance
under_checked.risk: LOW — D8 is documented contract (not Pydantic schema)

under_checked.reason: No Phase 3 agent focused on test strategy verification
under_checked.risk: LOW — test coverage metrics referenced elsewhere

under_checked.reason: Agent 9 covers CI/CD but does not directly verify B6 pipeline details

over_checked.note: 3 agents overlap on this file. This is acceptable given it contains LOCK-critical cost ceilings and safety policies.
2 agents overlap. Appropriate — contains the contentious L0 TTL internal conflict.

systematic_biases.description: Phase 3 agents 1-4 explicitly focus on V0-V3 version scoping. Agents 5-9 focus on PART2 subsections. This creates strong coverage of V0/V1 content but weaker coverage of V2/V3-only features.
systematic_biases.impact: LOW — V2/V3 features are future scope; current verification priorities are correct.

systematic_biases.description: Zero Phase 3 agents directly verified any STEP7 work guide or review file (30 files, 44% of SOT corpus). These contain V2/V3 enhancement specifications.
systematic_biases.impact: MEDIUM — Some STEP7 items may contain LOCK values or constraints that propagate into PART2 V2/V3 sections without verification.

systematic_biases.bias: Schema file vs Design file preference
systematic_biases.description: Agents prefer checking D2.0-XX design documents over D2.1-XX schema documents. D2.0 has 7/8 files checked, D2.1 has 5/10 files checked.
systematic_biases.impact: LOW — Schema documents are derivatives of design docs; field counts are verified via PART2 tables.

systematic_biases.description: P3 (omission) accounts for 30/45 (66.7%) of Phase 2 findings. Phase 3 agents continue this pattern with 'missing' type at 34/61 (55.7%). This may reflect genuine PART2 behavior (implementation guide naturally omits details) rather than agent bias.
systematic_biases.impact: LOW — Omission is expected in an implementation guide that references SOT for details.

phase3.note: Phase 3 agents 1-9 produced 67 findings total (agents 1-8: 61, agent 9: 6). P3 omission remains dominant pattern.

cross_phase_summary.P8_lock_violations: Phase 1: 1, Phase 2: 0, Phase 3: limited — LOCK/FREEZE compliance is strong
cross_phase_summary.P1_numeric_mismatches: Phase 1: 1, Phase 2: 5, Phase 3: 18 mismatch — consistently found across phases

dim_b_content.note: All phases extensively check content accuracy (field counts, values, terminology). This is the strongest dimension.
dim_b_content.agents: All agents 1-9 perform forward/reverse content verification

dim_c_implementation.note: Phase 3 agents 5-9 check implementation feasibility and code path mapping. Weaker than dim_b because no actual code exists yet — verification is against PHASE_B2 structure specs.

dim_d_prompt.note: Prompt-level verification (checking that PART2 prompt instructions would correctly guide AI code generation) is the weakest dimension. Only Agent 11 XREF analysis and Phase 2 adversarial checks touch this.
dim_d_prompt.agents: Indirect coverage only — no dedicated prompt verification agent

dimension_coverage.overall_assessment: B >> C > D. Dimension D (prompt quality) is systematically under-assessed across all phases.

phase0_cross_match_assessment.assessment: Phase 0 cross-match provides strong foundational SOT consistency verification. The 0.93% issue rate across 3236 comparisons indicates high SOT internal consistency. Enhanced validation (symbolic-verify, fact-audit, deep-diff, json-diff) all PASS.

findings.description: D2.0-04 (Infra Core) has zero dedicated Phase 3 agent verification. It defines BrainAdapter, RateLimitConfig, PromptCacheManager — all V1 critical components. Phase 2 Agents 1 checked some D2.0-04 content but no deep dive.
findings.recommendation: In future verification rounds, assign an agent specifically to D2.0-04 vs PART2 Infra Core sections.

findings.description: STEP7 file family (30 files, 44% of SOT corpus) has zero direct agent verification across all phases. While these are primarily V2/V3 enhancement specs, some contain LOCK values that flow into PART2.
findings.recommendation: For V2 verification cycle, add a STEP7-focused verification agent or expand Agent 8 scope.

findings.description: Dimension D (prompt quality) coverage is only ~25%. No agent systematically evaluates whether PART2's AI-directed instructions would produce correct implementation. The 4-Dimension framework promises B+C+D coverage but D is effectively unverified.
findings.recommendation: Add a dedicated Dimension D agent that evaluates PART2 prompt instructions for ambiguity, completeness, and potential AI misinterpretation.

findings.description: P3 omission is the dominant error pattern across all phases (33% -> 42% -> 51%), with an increasing trend. This is structurally expected: PART2 is an implementation guide that deliberately defers details to SOT references. Most 'omissions' are by-reference design, not errors.
findings.recommendation: Future agents should distinguish between 'intentional omission with SOT reference' (acceptable) and 'omission without any reference' (defect). Current metrics conflate these.

findings.description: Phase 3 quality is high: 139 facts checked across 9 agents, 22 mismatches found (15.8% mismatch rate). Phase 2 adversarial review correctly downgraded many Phase 1 HIGH findings to LOW/MEDIUM. The pipeline is self-correcting.
findings.recommendation: No action needed — pipeline health is good.

findings.description: D2.0-07 (Safety/Cost) is checked by 3 agents across phases. While justified by its LOCK-critical content, this creates redundant findings (e.g., L2 Guardrails role description appears in Phase 2 F-06, Phase 3 Agent 7 F-A7-01, and Agent 5 CostGate findings).
findings.recommendation: Implement finding deduplication at cross-agent level to avoid repeated flagging of the same issue.

findings.description: Phase 0 CM (cross-match) results (3236 comparisons, 0.93% issue rate) are not systematically consumed by Phase 3 agents. CM identified 16 CRITICAL issues and 2 INCONSISTENT references in CM-08, but Phase 3 agents do not cross-reference these.
findings.recommendation: Future agents should ingest CM results as pre-validated baselines and focus on areas where CM flagged issues.

recommendations.action: Add Dimension D (prompt quality) verification agent
recommendations.rationale: Currently 25% coverage on the weakest dimension. PART2 is primarily consumed by AI — its prompt clarity is mission-critical.
Only unchecked D2.0 design document. Contains V1-critical BrainAdapter and rate limiting specs.
Same issues flagged by multiple agents/phases (e.g., L2 Guardrails, CostGate thresholds). Dedup would improve signal-to-noise.

recommendations.action: Distinguish intentional vs unintentional omissions in P3 classification
recommendations.rationale: P3 dominance (51% of Phase 3 findings) inflates defect counts. Many are by-design references to SOT.

recommendations.action: Plan STEP7 verification for V2 cycle
recommendations.rationale: 30 files (44% of SOT) unchecked. Low urgency for V0/V1 but needed before V2 implementation.

overall_assessment.verification_depth: Phase 1 (16 findings) -> Phase 2 (45 findings) -> Phase 3 (67 findings) shows increasing depth with appropriate severity calibration
overall_assessment.self_correction: Phase 2 adversarial review successfully downgraded 7 Phase 1 HIGH findings, demonstrating pipeline self-correction
overall_assessment.coverage_adequacy: Core SOT files (24/68 = 35%) cover 98.5% of PART2 content by reference weight. The unchecked 44 files are supplementary/future-scope.
overall_assessment.key_risk: Dimension D (prompt quality) is the primary blind spot. PART2 will be consumed by AI agents — its instructional clarity directly affects implementation quality.
